---
title: A Sample Book Using Quarto with Standard LaTeX Book Class
format:
  xbook-en-pdf:
    keep-tex: true
author:
  - id: xz
    name: Your Name
    affiliations:
      - name: Your University
        department: Your Department
        city: Your City
        country: Your Country
        postal-code: 000000
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0001
    email: your.email@university.edu
    url: https://your-university.edu/your-profile
    attributes:
      corresponding: true
    bio: |
      Author biography text goes here.
  - name: John Doe
    affiliations:
      - name: Anonymous University
    bio: |
      John Doe is a researcher at Anonymous University.
abstract: |
  This document demonstrates how to use Quarto with the standard LaTeX book
  document class. It produces PDF output suitable for monographs,
  dissertations, and long-form academic writing.
keywords: [Quarto, LaTeX, book, Pandoc, template]
bibliography: bibliography.bib
date: 2023-06-23
---

# Introduction {#sec-intro}

This file is a sample book demonstrating Quarto's capabilities with the standard
LaTeX `book` document class. It supports PDF (via LaTeX) output.
It is assumed that the reader has a basic working knowledge of stochastic calculus @karatzas1991brownian
and stochastic control theory @yong1999sch.

::: {#thm-residue .theorem}
## Residue Theorem
Let $f$ be analytic in a simply connected domain $D$. Then for any closed contour $\gamma \subset D$,
$$
\oint_\gamma f(z)\,dz = 2\pi i \sum_{k} \mathrm{Res}(f, z_k)
$$
:::

This template supports standard Quarto cross-references. You can refer to Theorem [@thm-residue] in the text.

# Markdown Basics {#sec-markdown}

The template is built on Quarto and Pandoc, leveraging standard LaTeX tools for PDF output.

## Equations

Inline math: $E = mc^2$. Display math:

$$
\frac{\partial u}{\partial t} = \alpha \nabla^2 u
$$

## Theorems and Proofs

::: {#lem-key .lemma}
## Key Lemma
If $f$ is continuous on $[a,b]$, then $f$ is integrable on $[a,b]$.
:::

::: {#cor-easy .corollary}
## Easy Corollary
Every continuous function on a closed interval is bounded.
:::

::: {#def-model .definition}
## Model Definition
A stochastic process $\{X_t\}_{t \ge 0}$ is a collection of random variables indexed by time.
:::

## Lists

1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item

- Bullet one
- Bullet two

## Tables

| Variable | Description | Unit |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| $x$       | Position    | m    |
| $v$       | Velocity    | m/s  |
| $a$       | Acceleration| m/s² |

: A sample table {#tbl-sample}

# Bibliography

This template uses `natbib` with the `plainnat-doi` bibliography style for author-year citations @karatzas1991brownian.

# Acknowledgment {-}

This should be a simple paragraph before the References to
thank those individuals and institutions who have supported
your work on this book.

[]{.appendix options="An Appendix"}

Use the `[]{.appendix options="An Appendix"}` markup if you have a single appendix.
Do not use `\section{}` after `\appendix` in LaTeX.
